event: Completed a three-dose induction course of infliximab
Treated with IV corticosteroids and another dose of infliximab
Computed tomography (CT) showed multiple multi-loculated abscesses in the right hepatic lobe
CT showed extensive intrahepatic thrombosis of the right portal vein
CT showed diffuse colonic wall thickening
Abscess was initially drained by interventional radiology
Cultures were positive for Peptostreptococci, skin flora, and Escherichia coli
Patient was bacteremic with Streptococcus viridans
Patient was bacteremic with E. coli
Repeat wound cultures showed the same organisms
Antimicrobial therapy was changed from vancomycin and meropenem to ceftriaxone and metronidazole
Repeat CT showed continued improvement in size of the abscesses
Therapy was de-escalated to oral amoxicillin-clavulanate
Completed a 2-week course of oral amoxicillin-clavulanate
Liver loculations improved and resolved on ultrasonography
Normalization of liver enzymes and inflammatory markers
Remained without signs or symptoms of UC
